name: CodeX Live
tagline: AI-powered collaborative coding platform designed for developers and teams.
problem: Collaborative coding tools are typically disconnected from debugging engines and AI reviews, requiring engineers to constantly switch windows between development, terminal consoles, and ChatGPT. This context-switching breaks developer flow and delays feedback loops during peer programming sessions.
goals: Real-time synchronized document sharing across multiple clients
Instant AI code reviews and optimization tips right in the editor
Sandboxed environment to run programs directly in the browser
High-performance state replication with zero lag cursor tracking

architecture.frontend: React, Monaco Code Editor, Socket.io Client, CSS3, Tailwind
architecture.backend: Node.js, Express, Socket.io Server Gateway, Code Execution Sandbox Proxy
architecture.database: MongoDB Atlas for workspace state storing and profiles metadata
architecture.communication: Bidirectional web socket events synchronizing cursors, text selections, document diffs, and live workspace chat signals.

Collaboration: Team workspace live chat and presence indicators
AI Code Assistant: Inline AI reviews with Google Gemini
Bug detection and structural warnings alerts

challenges.title: Socket State Merging Conflicts
challenges.description: When multiple developers write concurrently on the same line, network latency causes cursor drifting and text overlap, corrupting document buffers.
challenges.solution: Implemented a debounced diff-patch sequence tracking edits. Document edits are serialized as light transaction diff operations, resolved sequentially on the Socket.io backend, and broadcast as unified snapshots back to the nodes.

challenges.title: Secure In-Browser Script Execution
challenges.description: Allowing arbitrary scripts execution on the host server creates high security risks (unlimited loops, shell escape scripts).
challenges.solution: Engineered a containerized execution sandbox that routes execution payloads to isolated runtime environments with memory limitations and short execution timeouts.

decisions.reason: Monaco provides the identical industrial-grade editor engine powering VS Code, yielding a high-quality coding UI with native syntax highlights and keyboard hooks.
Guarantees low-latency, duplex state synchronizations with automatic reconnect fallback systems to combat shaky network connections.

lessons: Building robust offline sync caches is crucial to prevent document state losses.
Optimizing socket frame payloads by compressing diff payloads significantly decreases client network bandwidth usage.
results: Achieved seamless 60fps real-time updates for document edits and cursor tracking.
Reduced local program compilation setup times by moving execution sandboxes completely into the cloud.
Completed a successful capstone prototype that solves remote team pair-programming context shifts.

name: CipherSQL Studio
tagline: Secure web client and relational database visual management platform.
problem: Traditional database administrators rely on desktop SQL clients that are heavy, lack dynamic relational visualization, and fail to secure connection configurations. Non-technical staff find it difficult to analyze complex schema keys and query database metrics easily.
goals: Zero-install SQL editor with auto-completes and quick execution
Dynamic visual mapping of relational foreign keys and table links
Secure credential stores preserving databases config tokens
Comprehensive telemetry dashboard documenting transaction speeds

architecture.frontend: React SPA, interactive node graphs, Tailwind styling layout
architecture.backend: Node/Express secure proxy forwarding parsed SQL commands
architecture.auth: Encryption keys + JWT authentication tokens for connection records
architecture.communication: Secure REST API communication routing database configurations with payload encryptions.

SQL Interface: In-browser SQL editor with query formatters
Paginated results table and export options
Saved history lists of executed queries
Relational Maps: Visual schema explorer detailing column types

challenges.title: Translating Relational Coordinates
challenges.description: Dynamically converting dynamic relational tables schema coordinates into logical nodes that don't overlap.
challenges.solution: Integrated a graph rendering algorithm that parses raw database table primary-foreign configurations and computes balanced layout coordinates automatically.

challenges.title: Securing Database Connection Records
challenges.description: Preserving database credentials on the cloud without exposing passwords to cross-site scripting (XSS).
challenges.solution: Implemented AES-256 encryption on credentials storage records. Decryption handles run exclusively in-memory on the server and keys are shielded behind environment boundaries.

decisions.reason: Provides interactive layout hooks, custom link colors, and dynamic resizing to support larger enterprise schemas without canvas lag.
Its robust database system supports parsing native schema catalogs, tables, and keys metadata cleanly.

lessons: Strict data sanitization and parameterized queries are critical to protect connections from SQL injections.
Caching connection pools on the proxy dramatically speeds up table loading.
results: Created a zero-install database client capable of visually resolving schemas in under 500ms.
Delivered a dashboard interface that simplifies database telemetry for non-experts.

name: DevFolio
tagline: AI-powered developer platform combining portfolio analytics, resume builders, and career guidance.
problem: Developers struggle to manage fragmented profiles across GitHub, LeetCode, and job boards. Generating tailored resumes for diverse applications and tracking job pipelines manually leads to lost time, outdated metrics, and unguided career pathways.
goals: Integrate real-time GitHub APIs for dynamic coding profile analytics dashboards
Provide automated ATS resume optimization guides powered by Gemini AI
Map specialized interactive learning roadmaps for target tech stacks
Unify job application pipeline tracking and interviews calendar under one view

architecture.frontend: React 18 SPA, Recharts visualizations, Lucide icons, Framer Motion animations
architecture.backend: Client-side API service layer connecting directly to Gemini AI and GitHub REST APIs
architecture.database: Zustand persistent local stores caching job pipelines, resume templates, and logs
architecture.auth: Local client profiles setup + localStorage preference caching
architecture.communication: Direct Axios HTTP queries requesting structured streaming tokens from Gemini AI models

Resume & Job Tracker: ATS keywords check & resume templates builder
Interactive Kanban board job application pipeline tracker
Analytics Panel: Coding logs and visual habits charts via Recharts

challenges.title: Gemini Stream Handling in React
challenges.description: Streaming tokens from Gemini API created frequent state redraw cycles, slowing down UI transitions and locking browser inputs.
challenges.solution: Designed a debounced stream collector buffer. Stream chunk arrays are stored in custom refs and flushed to the Zustand state store in controlled batch intervals, maintaining 60fps animations.

challenges.title: Centralized State Sync
challenges.description: Syncing complex profile setups, resume structures, and job boards led to state drift and storage latency.
challenges.solution: Implemented Zustand slices architecture, separating the state into atomic blocks with localStorage persistence middlewares.

decisions.reason: Provides a lightweight, developer-friendly boilerplate-free state manager that avoids the performance issues of React Context under high-frequency updates.
Uses declarative React components that adapt dynamically to responsive container sizes and dark mode variables.

lessons: Provider-based abstraction layers make switching between Gemini and other LLM models simple.
Debouncing React state updates during active API text streaming is crucial to keep transitions fluid.
results: Created a fully unified career hub dashboard reducing portfolio setup times.
Achieved 100/100 performance marks by avoiding server-side database latency.

name: Matty Graphic Design Tool
tagline: Interactive design and whiteboarding tool for graphics templates.
problem: Designing and sharing team marketing templates internally required heavy graphic editor installations. Non-designers struggled to adapt templates, leading to visual deviations, inconsistent assets, and delays in marketing campaigns.
goals: Custom vector drawing board operating directly inside the browser
Persistent cloud workspace to save, edit, and export visual works
Structured templates directory for pre-set layouts
Team authentication systems separating personal and shared files

architecture.frontend: React SPA, Excalidraw custom integration wrapper, Tailwind
architecture.backend: Node.js/Express API routing template saves and workspace states
architecture.database: MongoDB schemas cataloging canvas states, coordinates, and team nodes
architecture.communication: Standard REST APIs sending serialized JSON canvas arrays to databases.

Canvas Board: Dynamic shape drawing, text layers, and custom assets upload
Template Center: Shared directory with pre-styled graphic presets

challenges.title: Saving Complex Vector Coordinates
challenges.description: Saving canvas shapes led to massive JSON documents, causing heavy database load and network lag on save triggers.
challenges.solution: Developed a custom compressor filtering out unchanged vector properties (e.g. redundant viewport coordinates), shrinking the save payload by 75%.

challenges.title: Ensuring Offline Canvas Recovery
challenges.description: Network drops during template updates resulted in lost changes and corrupted workspace files.
challenges.solution: Implemented a dual-storage strategy. Changes are saved instantly to local storage and synchronized with the database in background intervals using a retry queue.

decisions.reason: Provides a clean, hand-drawn vector sketch style, supports custom tools, and has a lightweight drawing engine.
Significantly optimized hot-module replacement and compilation times for drawing canvas bundles compared to standard bundlers.

lessons: Decoupling canvas state managers from React render loops prevents redraw lag.
Dynamic background auto-saves greatly improve UX and security.
results: Successfully integrated custom template editors into GNCIPL team marketing flows.
Maintained 60fps canvas performance on heavy template edits.
Delivered a production-ready application to streamline team design tasks.

name: DocChat
tagline: AI-powered document assistant for dynamic conversational Q&A on PDFs.
problem: Reading, analyzing, and extracting data from long academic or financial documents is tedious. Existing search tools only locate exact keyword matches, failing to understand semantic context or answer conceptual questions.
goals: AI PDF layout parsing and unstructured text extraction
Semantic conversational assistant answering prompts directly from documents
Persistent chat history lists linked to user workspaces
Responsive, clean file dropzone interface

architecture.frontend: React SPA with dynamic chat layouts and scroll triggers
architecture.backend: Node/Express parser processing PDF arrays and routing embeddings
architecture.database: MongoDB archiving history metadata and text references
architecture.communication: REST API routing parsed document chunks and chat payloads to Google Gemini.

AI Interface: Interactive citation links pointing to source text segments

challenges.title: Context Window Exhaustion
challenges.description: Passing entire PDF text to the LLM model exceeds context token limits and increases API latency.
challenges.solution: Developed a text-chunking system. The backend splits PDFs into overlap blocks, compares query embeddings using local vector heuristics, and forwards only the relevant context block to the Gemini API.

challenges.title: Large PDF Processing Failures
challenges.description: Processing PDFs over 50 pages caused API timeout errors on serverless endpoints.
challenges.solution: Implemented chunked stream processing. Text parsing runs in stages, showing a progress indicator, and stores text references as document indexes.

decisions.reason: Offers large context windows, fast response times, and powerful text extraction capabilities compared to other providers.
Enabled fast layout prototyping for chat message boxes, drawers, and drag-and-drop widgets.

lessons: Implementing overlaps in text chunking is vital to prevent losing context at split points.
Pre-extracting document structures speeds up response times.
results: Enabled users to question long PDF materials, getting contextual answers in under 1 second.
Maintained low server resource usage by outsourcing AI processing.

name: Wanderlust
tagline: Full-stack travel booking application simulating vacation rentals.
problem: Vacation listing platforms require complex coordination of listings, reviews, geographical mapping, and image uploads. Building a reliable server-side MVC architecture to handle these inputs securely is a core software engineering challenge.
goals: Comprehensive CRUD operations for listing properties
Interactive map visualizers showing vacation positions
Clean user reviews, ratings, and feedback loops
Reliable image storage and user sessions

architecture.frontend: EJS server-side rendering layouts, Bootstrap styling elements
architecture.backend: Node.js, Express framework routing MVC controller logic
architecture.database: MongoDB Atlas storing listings, user records, and review schemas
architecture.auth: Cookie sessions and passport local authentication modules
architecture.communication: Server-side template injection rendering Mapbox API maps and listing metadata.

Listing Portal: Create, read, update, and delete vacation listings
Image uploads with automatic Cloudinary storage
Price, location, and description details management
Interactions: Secure user reviews and rating stars
Map layouts highlighting exact location pins
Authentication checks limiting edits to owners

challenges.title: Cloudinary CDN Sync issues
challenges.description: Updating or deleting listings left orphaned image files in Cloudinary, wasting cloud storage allocation.
challenges.solution: Integrated Mongoose database middlewares. Deleting a database listing triggers a hook that extracts the asset public ID and deletes it from Cloudinary.

challenges.title: Parsing Location Pins on Server side
challenges.description: Geocoding location names (e.g. 'Paris') into map coordinates requires API lookups that slow page rendering.
challenges.solution: Implemented pre-geocoding. Location names are parsed via Mapbox Geocoding API on listing creation, saving lat-long coordinates directly to the document.

decisions.reason: Keeps routing, controller actions, and database schemas separated for clean codebase organization.
Allows fast HTML rendering on the server side, keeping page assets lightweight and improving performance.

lessons: Always design database schemas with cascading delete rules to keep data clean.
Input validation on both client and server sides is essential for data integrity.
results: Completed a robust clone demonstrating server-side MVC capabilities.
Deployed application to Render with live image uploads and mapping services.
